|---------|--------|

| "I know the file name" | You might not know the best match |
| "Glob is faster for this" | Only if you have an exact filename pattern |
| "This is a simple search" | Simple searches benefit most from semantic |

If semantic search is unavailable, Grep/Glob are acceptable fallbacks.
</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>"}}

    14→package tsdb

    16→import (

    18→	"crypto/rand"
    19→	"errors"
    20→	"fmt"
    21→	"io"
    22→	"log/slog"

    31→

    35→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/fileutil"
    36→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/index"
    37→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/tombstones"
    38→)
    39→
    40→// ExponentialBlockRanges returns the time ranges based on the stepSize.
    41→func ExponentialBlockRanges(minSize int64, steps, stepSize int) []int64 {
    42→	ranges := make([]int64, 0, steps)
    43→	curRange := minSize
    44→	for range steps {
    45→		ranges = append(ranges, curRange)
    46→		curRange *= int64(stepSize)
    47→	}
    48→
    49→	return ranges
    50→}
    51→
    52→// Compactor provides compaction against an underlying storage
    53→// of time series data.
    54→type Compactor interface {
    55→	// Plan returns a set of directories that can be compacted concurrently.
    56→	// The directories can be overlapping.
    57→	// Results returned when compactions are in progress are undefined.
    58→	Plan(dir string) ([]string, error)
    59→
    60→	// Write persists one or more Blocks into a directory.
    61→	// No Block is written when resulting Block has 0 samples and returns an empty slice.
    62→	// Prometheus always return one or no block. The interface allows returning more than one
    63→	// block for downstream users to experiment with compactor.
    64→	Write(dest string, b BlockReader, mint, maxt int64, base *BlockMeta) ([]ulid.ULID, error)
    65→
    66→	// Compact runs compaction against the provided directories. Must
    67→	// only be called concurrently with results of Plan().
    68→	// Can optionally pass a list of already open blocks,
    69→	// to avoid having to reopen them.
    70→	// Prometheus always return one or no block. The interface allows returning more than one
    71→	// block for downstream users to experiment with compactor.
    72→	// When one resulting Block has 0 samples
    73→	//  * No block is written.
    74→	//  * The source dirs are marked Deletable.
    75→	//  * Block is not included in the result.
    76→	Compact(dest string, dirs []string, open []*Block) ([]ulid.ULID, error)
    77→}
    78→
    79→// LeveledCompactor implements the Compactor interface.
    80→type LeveledCompactor struct {
    81→	metrics                     *CompactorMetrics
    82→	logger                      *slog.Logger
    83→	ranges                      []int64
    84→	chunkPool                   chunkenc.Pool
    85→	ctx                         context.Context
    86→	maxBlockChunkSegmentSize    int64
    87→	useUncachedIO               bool
    88→	mergeFunc                   storage.VerticalChunkSeriesMergeFunc
    89→	blockExcludeFunc            BlockExcludeFilterFunc
    90→	postingsEncoder             index.PostingsEncoder
    91→	postingsDecoderFactory      PostingsDecoderFactory
    92→	enableOverlappingCompaction bool
    93→}
    94→
    95→type CompactorMetrics struct {
    96→	Ran               prometheus.Counter
    97→	PopulatingBlocks  prometheus.Gauge
    98→	OverlappingBlocks prometheus.Counter
    99→	Duration          prometheus.Histogram
   100→	ChunkSize         prometheus.Histogram

    13→
    14→// Package tsdb implements a time series storage for float64 sample data.
    15→package tsdb
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"context"
    19→	"errors"
    20→	"fmt"
    21→	"io"
    22→	"io/fs"
    23→	"log/slog"
    24→	"math"
    25→	"math/rand"
    26→	"os"
    27→	"path/filepath"
    28→	"slices"
    29→	"strings"
    30→	"sync"
    31→	"time"
    32→
    33→	"github.com/oklog/ulid/v2"
    34→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
    35→	"github.com/prometheus/common/promslog"
    36→	"go.uber.org/atomic"
    37→	"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup"
    38→
    39→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/config"
    40→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels"
    41→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage"
    42→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/chunkenc"
    43→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/chunks"
    44→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/fileutil"
    45→	_ "github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/goversion" // Load the package into main to make sure minimum Go version is met.
    46→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/tsdbutil"
    47→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/wlog"
    48→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/compression"
    49→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/features"
    50→	prom_runtime "github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/runtime"
    51→)
    52→
    53→const (
    54→	// DefaultBlockDuration in milliseconds.
    55→	DefaultBlockDuration = int64(2 * time.Hour / time.Millisecond)
    56→
    57→	// DefaultCompactionDelayMaxPercent in percentage.
    58→	DefaultCompactionDelayMaxPercent = 10
    59→
    60→	// Block dir suffixes to make deletion and creation operations atomic.
    61→	// We decided to do suffixes instead of creating meta.json as last (or delete as first) one,
    62→	// because in error case you still can recover meta.json from the block content within local TSDB dir.
    63→	// TODO(bwplotka): TSDB can end up with various .tmp files (e.g meta.json.tmp, WAL or segment tmp file. Think
    64→	// about removing those too on start to save space. Currently only blocks tmp dirs are removed.
    65→	tmpForDeletionBlockDirSuffix = ".tmp-for-deletion"
    66→	tmpForCreationBlockDirSuffix = ".tmp-for-creation"
    67→	// Pre-2.21 tmp dir suffix, used in clean-up functions.
    68→	tmpLegacy = ".tmp"
    69→)
    70→
    71→// ErrNotReady is returned if the underlying storage is not ready yet.
    72→var ErrNotReady = errors.New("TSDB not ready")
    73→
    74→// DefaultOptions used for the DB. They are reasonable for setups using
    75→// millisecond precision timestamps.
    76→func DefaultOptions() *Options {
    77→	return &Options{
    78→		WALSegmentSize:              wlog.DefaultSegmentSize,
    79→		MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize:    chunks.DefaultChunkSegmentSize,
    80→		RetentionDuration:           int64(15 * 24 * time.Hour / time.Millisecond),
    81→		MinBlockDuration:            DefaultBlockDuration,
    82→		MaxBlockDuration:            DefaultBlockDuration,
    83→		NoLockfile:                  false,
    84→		SamplesPerChunk:             DefaultSamplesPerChunk,
    85→		WALCompression:              compression.None,
    86→		StripeSize:                  DefaultStripeSize,
    87→		HeadChunksWriteBufferSize:   chunks.DefaultWriteBufferSize,
    88→		IsolationDisabled:           defaultIsolationDisabled,
    89→		HeadChunksWriteQueueSize:    chunks.DefaultWriteQueueSize,
    90→		OutOfOrderCapMax:            DefaultOutOfOrderCapMax,
    91→		EnableOverlappingCompaction: true,
    92→		EnableSharding:              false,
    93→		EnableDelayedCompaction:     false,
    94→		CompactionDelayMaxPercent:   DefaultCompactionDelayMaxPercent,
    95→		CompactionDelay:             time.Duration(0),
    96→		PostingsDecoderFactory:      DefaultPostingsDecoderFactory,
    97→		BlockReloadInterval:         1 * time.Minute,
    98→	}
    99→}
   100→
   101→// Options of the DB storage.
   102→type Options struct {
   103→	// staleSeriesCompactionThreshold is same as below option with same name, but is atomic so that we can do live updates without locks.
   104→	// This is the one that must be used by the code.
   105→	staleSeriesCompactionThreshold atomic.Float64
   106→
   107→	// Segments (wal files) max size.
   108→	// WALSegmentSize = 0, segment size is default size.
   109→	// WALSegmentSize > 0, segment size is WALSegmentSize.
   110→	// WALSegmentSize < 0, wal is disabled.
   111→	WALSegmentSize int
   112→
   113→	// MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize is the max size of block chunk segment files.
   114→	// MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize = 0, chunk segment size is default size.
   115→	// MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize > 0, chunk segment size is MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize.
   116→	MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize int64
   117→
   118→	// Duration of persisted data to keep.
   119→	// Unit agnostic as long as unit is consistent with MinBlockDuration and MaxBlockDuration.
   120→	// Typically it is in milliseconds.
   121→	RetentionDuration int64
   122→
   123→	// Maximum number of bytes in blocks to be retained.
   124→	// 0 or less means disabled.
   125→	// NOTE: For proper storage calculations need to consider
   126→	// the size of the WAL folder which is not added when calculating
   127→	// the current size of the database.
   128→	MaxBytes int64
   129→
   130→	// Maximum % of disk space to use for blocks to be retained.
   131→	// 0 or less means disabled.
   132→	// If both MaxBytes and MaxPercentage are set, percentage prevails.
   133→	MaxPercentage uint
   134→
   135→	// NoLockfile disables creation and consideration of a lock file.
   136→	NoLockfile bool
   137→
   138→	// WALCompression configures the compression type to use on records in the WAL.
   139→	WALCompression compression.Type
   140→
   141→	// Maximum number of CPUs that can simultaneously processes WAL replay.
   142→	// If it is <=0, then GOMAXPROCS is used.
   143→	WALReplayConcurrency int
   144→
   145→	// StripeSize is the size in entries of the series hash map. Reducing the size will save memory but impact performance.
   146→	StripeSize int
   147→
   148→	// The timestamp range of head blocks after which they get persisted.
   149→	// It's the minimum duration of any persisted block.
   150→	// Unit agnostic as long as unit is consistent with RetentionDuration and MaxBlockDuration.
